free_text: Energy dependence of the three-nucleon transfer
reactions on light nuclei.
An apparatus to
monitor mean energy of beam.
A Moplefan (C(3)H(4))n foil 1.1 mg/cm2 thick was used
as the carbon target. Gas targets were used for the
measurements on N-15 and O-16. The nitrogen gas was
enriched to 99.5% in the isotope N-15, the oxygen gas
was the natural isotopic mixture. The gas target
consisted of a 70-mm-diam cylindrical cell, with two
windows covered by 2-mg/cm2-thick Havar foils, each
15 mm high and extending over 150 degree, filled to
gas pressures in the range 15-30 cm Hg.
700 um-thick surface-barrier silicon detector.
A distorted-wave born-approximation
calculations. The pre-equilibrium decay model.
Correction according to MEMO CP-D/294.
additional comments are added. ERR-ANALYS lines are
corrected too.
Date is corrected
Subents O0323.044-045 are corrected
according to O.Schwerer remarks.
Data-heading in Subents 002-042 is
corrected according N.Otsuka remarks.
Last checking has been done.

- by compiler - what energy group has been investigated
is unclear. Probably 2.37 MeV level of N-13 production
was investigated.  But the production of 4 levels of
N-13 could not be excluded too.
Data point density is very high on the figure.
Therefore some data points could be omitted.

The data points were produced as sum
sigma(theta)*sin(theta) of differential cross sections
at 35, 75, 125 and 165 degrees. This was normalized to
the integrated cross section deduced from the angular
distributions obtained at a number of energies.
